name: Kathi Roll of Nizam
recipeIngredient: 6 medium Potatoes - Boiled, peeled, and mashed
1 teaspoon Green chili - Seedless and chopped
Ghee or oil for shallow frying
recipeInstructions:
1. Sieve the flour and 2 teaspoons of salt into a bowl.
2. Rub in 2 tablespoons of ghee or oil.
3. Gradually add warm water, mixing well until a soft dough forms.
4. Place the dough on a well-greased surface and cover with a damp cloth.
5. Allow the dough to rest for 20 minutes.
6. Knead again and divide into nine equal parts.
7. Shape each part into round balls.
8. Roll out each ball into a round disc measuring 7 inches (18 cm) in diameter.
9. Heat a griddle and place a roti on it.
10. Drizzle 1-2 teaspoons of ghee or oil around the edges, ensuring they remain soft.
11. Mix mashed potatoes with salt, chopped green chili, and coriander leaves.
12. Divide the mixture into nine parts and stuff each roti accordingly.
13. Roll up each stuffed roti like a Swiss roll.
14. Secure with a cocktail stick (toothpick).
15. Wrap each roll in 4-inch x 4-inch pieces of butter paper (wax paper), covering it halfway.
16. Serve with mint chutney.
Note: Instead of potatoes, boiled and mashed eggs, minced meat, or boiled and ground chicken can be used for stuffing. Cooking time: 35 minutes.
